1	"""The measurement ↔ interpretation fence (METHODOLOGY.md §6).
2	
3	A hard boundary separates **measurement** (numbers the engine produces) from
4	**interpretation** (what they mean). Interpretation lives strictly downstream and cannot
5	feed back into measurement. Any use of judgment — including LLM-assisted classification — is
6	interpretation, and is labeled as such.
7	
8	These wrappers make the boundary explicit and type-visible in the pipeline. ``fence`` turns
9	a measurement into an interpretation; there is deliberately no inverse — you cannot turn an
10	interpretation back into a measurement.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	from collections.abc import Mapping as AbcMapping
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from typing import Any, Generic, Mapping, TypeVar
18	
19	from assay_engine._frozen import duck_mapping_items, freeze, freeze_mapping
20	
21	T = TypeVar("T")
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
25	class Interpretation(Generic[T]):
26	    """A judgment *about* one or more measurements. Downstream-only; never an input upstream."""
27	
28	    value: T
29	    basis: tuple[str, ...]  # refs (producer:inputs_hash) of the measurements this interprets
30	    rationale: str
31	    judged_by: str  # e.g. "operator" or a model identifier — judgment is always labeled
32	    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
33	
34	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
35	        # Freeze the payload so the frozen record is deeply immutable + hashable (issue #29).
36	        object.__setattr__(self, "value", freeze(self.value))
37	        object.__setattr__(self, "metadata", freeze_mapping(self.metadata))
38	
39	
40	def _contains_interpretation(obj: Any) -> bool:
41	    """True if ``obj`` is, or recursively contains, an :class:`Interpretation`."""
42	    if isinstance(obj, Interpretation):
43	        return True
44	    if isinstance(obj, AbcMapping):
45	        # scan KEYS as well as values — an Interpretation used as a dict key would otherwise be
46	        # smuggled into a Measurement undetected (#115)
47	        return any(
48	            _contains_interpretation(k) or _contains_interpretation(v) for k, v in obj.items()
49	        )
50	    duck = duck_mapping_items(obj)
51	    if duck is not None:  # lazy/proxy dict-like — scan it like a mapping, not an opaque leaf (#140)
52	        return any(_contains_interpretation(k) or _contains_interpretation(v) for k, v in duck)
53	    if isinstance(obj, (list, tuple, set, frozenset)):
54	        return any(_contains_interpretation(v) for v in obj)
55	    return False
56	
57	
58	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
59	class Measurement(Generic[T]):
60	    """A value produced purely by the engine from data — no judgment involved."""
61	
62	    value: T
63	    produced_by: str
64	    inputs_hash: str
65	    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
66	
67	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
68	        # Structurally close the one-way fence: an interpretation may never re-enter as a
69	        # measurement value — at the top level OR nested in a container/metadata (issues #22,
70	        # #30). Note: ref/inputs_hash integrity (forgery resistance) is delegated to the
71	        # provenance/audit layer, not enforced here.
72	        if _contains_interpretation(self.value) or _contains_interpretation(self.metadata):
73	            raise TypeError(
74	                "Measurement value/metadata must not contain an Interpretation — "
75	                "interpretation must not feed back into measurement (the fence)"
76	            )
77	        # Freeze the payload so the frozen record is deeply immutable + hashable (issue #29).
78	        object.__setattr__(self, "value", freeze(self.value))
79	        object.__setattr__(self, "metadata", freeze_mapping(self.metadata))
80	
81	    @property
82	    def ref(self) -> str:
83	        """A stable id for this specific measurement: producer + input hash."""
84	        return f"{self.produced_by}:{self.inputs_hash}"
85	
86	
87	def fence(
88	    measurement: Measurement[Any], value: T, *, rationale: str, judged_by: str
89	) -> Interpretation[T]:
90	    """Cross the fence once, in the only permitted direction: measurement → interpretation.
91	
92	    ``basis`` records the specific measurement's ``ref`` (``producer:inputs_hash``), so an
93	    interpretation can be traced to the exact measurement it interprets — not merely to the
94	    producer (audit pass 1, issue #16).
95	    """
96	    return Interpretation(
97	        value=value,
98	        basis=(measurement.ref,),
99	        rationale=rationale,
100	        judged_by=judged_by,
101	        metadata={"inputs_hash": measurement.inputs_hash},
102	    )
103

6	- ``verdict``    — the three verdicts: supported / contradicted / indeterminate.
7	- ``firewalls``  — Firewall A (claim-blindness) and Firewall B (discover/confirm),
8	                   enforced as runtime primitives, not docstring promises.

44	
45	__all__ = [

47	    "Measurement",
48	    "fence",
49	    "ClaimBlindGuard",
50	    "DiscoverConfirmSplit",
51	    "FirewallViolation",
52	    "Hypothesis",

62	    "subset_corpus",
63	    "HeldOutConfirmer",
64	    "LocalHmacAuthority",
65	    "PreRegistrationError",
66	    "StampingAuthority",
67	    "TimestampAuthority",

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import logging
33	import time
34	import uuid
35	import warnings
36	from contextlib import ExitStack
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from pathlib import Path

85	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
86	class GateReview:
87	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
88	
89	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
90	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
91	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
92	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).
93	    """
94	
95	    gate: str
96	    frm: Phase
97	    to: Phase
98	    summary: str
99	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
100	
101	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
102	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
103	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
104	
105	    def payload_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	        """Return the payload as a plain, deeply-thawed, JSON-serializable ``dict`` (#141)."""
107	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(self.payload))

110	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
111	
112	
113	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
114	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137, #F-001).
115	
116	    Delegates to the public :func:`~assay_engine.contracts.claims.claim_set_fingerprint` so the
117	    engine's authoritative fingerprint and the scheme adapters must implement in
118	    ``claim_fingerprint()`` are one and the same canonical function — the engine recomputes it
119	    over the materialized records (in scored order) and enforces agreement with the source's
120	    self-report, so an auditor re-deriving the fingerprint from the scored claims gets the value
121	    the trail records.
122	    """
123	    return claim_set_fingerprint(claim_records)
124	
125	
126	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
127	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
128	
129	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
130	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
131	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
132	    """
133	    seen: set[str] = set()
134	    dupes: list[str] = []
135	    for i in ids:
136	        if i in seen:
137	            dupes.append(i)
138	        seen.add(i)
139	    if dupes:
140	        raise FirewallViolation(
141	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
142	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
143	        )

145	
146	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
147	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
148	
149	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
150	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
151	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
152	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
153	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
154	    """
155	    return GateDecision(
156	        approved=True,
157	        gate=review.gate,
158	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
159	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
160	    )

162	
163	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
164	class StudyPlan:
165	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
166	
167	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
168	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
169	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
170	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
171	    definition).
172	    """
173	
174	    definition: StudyDefinition
175	    source: Path
176	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
177	    authority: TimestampAuthority
178	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
179	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
180	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
181	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
182	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None
183	
184	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
185	        modes = self.definition.modes
186	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
187	            missing = [
188	                n
189	                for n, v in (
190	                    ("split", self.split),
191	                    ("discover", self.discover),
192	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
193	                )
194	                if v is None
195	            ]
196	            if missing:
197	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
198	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
199	            missing = [
200	                n
201	                for n, v in (
202	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
203	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
204	                )
205	                if v is None
206	            ]
207	            if missing:
208	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
209	
210	
211	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
212	class StudyResult:
213	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
214	
215	    study: str
216	    corpus_fingerprint: str
217	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
218	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
219	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
220	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
221	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
222	    source_version: str | None = None
223	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
224	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
225	
226	    def persist_trail(self, path: str | Path) -> Path:
227	        """Write the hash-chained provenance trail to ``path`` as JSON and return it (#F-045).
228	
229	        ``run_study`` keeps the trail only in memory (``self.provenance``); a caller that needs a
230	        durable, re-verifiable audit trail across process exit must persist it. The written
231	        records round-trip through :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.from_records` and re-check via
232	        :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.verify_records`. Call ``tracker.log_artifact`` on the
233	        returned path to attach it to the experiment run.
234	        """
235	        import json
236	
237	        from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
238	
239	        out = Path(path)
240	        out.write_text(
241	            json.dumps(list(entries_to_records(list(self.provenance))), indent=2),
242	            encoding="utf-8",
243	        )
244	        return out
245	
246	
247	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
248	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
249	
250	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
251	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
252	
253	    return _log
254	
255	
256	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
257	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
258	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
259	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
260	
261	
262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343	
344	    trail.record(
345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
357	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
358	                },
359	            )
360	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
361	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
362	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
363	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
364	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
365	            _log.warning(
366	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
367	                "to the tracker for this study",
368	                type(exc).__name__,
369	                exc,
370	            )
371	
372	    ok = False
373	    started_at = time.monotonic()
374	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
375	    try:
376	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
377	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).

findings.title: Post-hoc direction selection (HARKing) for whole-corpus hypotheses locked with predicted_direction=None
findings.description: _resolve_direction accepts a confirm-time predicted_direction argument whenever the locked hypothesis has predicted_direction=None. Nothing forces a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis to carry a non-None predicted_direction at lock/pre-registration time: Hypothesis.__post_init__ explicitly allows None, canonical_hypothesis_digest binds None into the proof, and require_preregistered happily verifies a None-direction lock. The result: a study can pre-register a directionless whole-corpus hypothesis, look at the observed statistic / baseline, then choose 'greater' or 'less' at confirm_whole_corpus call time to land in whichever tail is significant. This is textbook HARKing — choosing the predicted tail after seeing the data — and it defeats the entire point of fixing direction at lock time (the issue #24 the docstring claims to have closed). The pre-registration cryptographic binding gives no protection because the thing that was locked is literally 'no direction'. Both the supported and contradicted verdicts hinge on this freely-chosen direction (direction vs opposite tail), so an analyst gets to pick the verdict's polarity post-hoc.

from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisKind, HypothesisOrigin
from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import LocalHmacAuthority, lock_hypothesis
from assay_engine.methodology.confirm import confirm_whole_corpus
auth = LocalHmacAuthority(b'x'*16)
h = Hypothesis('h1','effect exists', HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY, 'perm','beats null', predicted_direction=None)  # NO direction locked
h = lock_hypothesis(h, authority=auth)   # proof binds predicted_direction=None
null = [float(i) for i in range(100)]
obs = 95.0
# analyst now sees obs is high, picks 'greater' to get supported:
v1 = confirm_whole_corpus(h, observed=obs, null_distribution=null, alpha=0.05, resample_statistics=[95.0]*20, predicted_direction='greater', authority=auth)
# had obs been low, analyst would pick 'less' instead — verdict polarity chosen post-hoc
print(v1.label)  # supported, direction chosen AFTER seeing data, pre-registration intact
findings.suggested_fix: Require predicted_direction to be set at lock time for WHOLE_CORPUS hypotheses. Either (a) in Hypothesis.__post_init__ raise if kind is WHOLE_CORPUS and predicted_direction is None, or (b) in _resolve_direction / confirm_whole_corpus reject locked_dir is None unconditionally (do not let a confirm-time argument supply the direction for a pre-registered hypothesis) — accept the confirm-time argument only for an unlocked exploratory hypothesis, never for one going through require_preregistered. The directionless-deferred path must not be reachable for a pre-registered confirmatory test.

findings.title: Contradicted verdict is reported without any stability requirement, asymmetric with supported
findings.description: In confirm_whole_corpus a 'supported' verdict requires both significance in the predicted tail AND stability >= threshold across resamples (engine-measured). A 'contradicted' verdict, however, is returned as soon as p_contra <= alpha, before stability is even consulted, and stability is only ever measured in the predicted (support) tail — never in the opposite tail. Consequence: a single noisy observed statistic that happens to fall in the opposite tail of the null yields a hard 'contradicted' verdict with no reproducibility check at all, while the symmetric 'supported' claim is held to a stability bar. This asymmetry means contradictions of external claims (the §5 source-scoring path) can be driven by non-reproducible noise, biasing alignment_rate downward (every unstable opposite-tail blip counts as n_contradicted in SourceScorecard). The docstring frames this as intentional ('reportable without stability') but it is a real methodological inconsistency: the engine demands reproduction for one verdict polarity and not the other.
findings.poc:
# A null centered at 0; one observed draw lands in the opposite tail by chance.
# confirm_whole_corpus returns CONTRADICTED with notes='significant in the opposite tail'
# without ever measuring whether that contradiction reproduces across resamples,
# whereas the same magnitude in the supportive tail would be gated by stability_threshold.
# In adjudication this contradiction increments scorecard.n_contradicted and lowers
# alignment_rate on the strength of a single unreproduced statistic.
findings.suggested_fix: Apply the stability requirement symmetrically: measure stability in whichever tail the verdict is being asserted (compute a contra-tail stability when p_contra<=alpha) and require it to clear the threshold before returning 'contradicted'; if resamples are absent, return 'indeterminate (stability not assessed)' for the contradiction too, exactly as is done for support. Alternatively, document explicitly in METHODOLOGY.md why contradictions are deliberately held to a weaker bar and surface the missing-stability caveat in the verdict evidence so the scorecard consumer can discount it.

findings.title: Direct confirm primitives default to presence-only pre-registration gate (authority=None) — bypassable real-verification
findings.description: _gate_preregistration falls back to require_locked (a pure presence check on locked_at/timestamp_proof) when authority is None, and authority defaults to None on both confirm_unit_level and confirm_whole_corpus. These primitives are study-facing (the adjudication docstring instructs studies to call them inside their confirm callables). A study confirmer that calls confirm_whole_corpus/confirm_unit_level without threading the authority through gets only the sentinel check, which a hand-written timestamp_proof='x' satisfies — i.e. no content-binding, no timestamp attestation, no lock-before-confirm ordering. In the composed run_study pipeline the runner pre-verifies via require_preregistered, so this is defense-in-depth there; but any study (or test/fixture) that drives these primitives directly, or a future runner that forgets to pass authority, silently downgrades pre-registration to a no-op. A safe-by-default API should require the authority, not make real verification opt-in.

findings.suggested_fix: Make authority a required keyword argument on confirm_unit_level and confirm_whole_corpus (no None default), so every confirmatory call performs full require_preregistered verification. If a deliberately unverified path must exist for exploratory use, gate it behind an explicit, loudly-named flag (e.g. verify_preregistration=False) rather than a silent None default, and forbid it for EXTERNAL_CLAIM/DISCOVERY origins.

findings.title: Unit-level confirm validates predicted_direction shape but never uses it, leaving direction_supports_claim fully unverified
findings.description: confirm_unit_level re-validates that hypothesis.predicted_direction (if present) is in {'greater','less'} as defense-in-depth, but the unit-level verdict is decided entirely by the trusted caller flag direction_supports_claim and never cross-checks it against the pre-registered predicted_direction. This is a smaller HARKing/consistency gap than M-1 (it requires a dishonest caller flag rather than free post-hoc choice) but the locked direction provides zero decision-time guarantee for the unit-level path.
